name: Addiction — Cue-Triggered Craving and Relapse
description: Environmental cues associated with past substance use trigger intense craving through conditioned associations, overwhelming conscious intentions to abstain.

context.environment: bar district, walking home from work via old route
context.culturalNorms: drinking is normalized in this social circle

beliefs: I can control it this time
I've been good for 3 months, I deserve a break
history: used to drink at bars on this street every Friday

stimulus.description: Walks past familiar bar, hears music, smells beer, sees old friends through the window laughing

expected.behavior: slows down, rationalizes ('just one drink'), enters the bar

sources: Robinson & Berridge 1993 — The Neural Basis of Drug Craving
